scope: Read-only metadata and local-image audit of Wikimedia Commons release candidates before publication.
audit_notes: Flags are model-association specific. A candidate shared with a correct model may remain archived after the wrong association is removed.
Foreign photo location, repaint, modifications, and event or capture dates were not grounds for rejection by themselves.
Ambiguous Monza, Cruze, HHR, Tilt Cab, Eagle commercial-body, and Express cutaway candidates were visually spot-checked and retained.
The Classic Six calendar-year conflict is flagged because the catalog explicitly says 1912 launch material must not become a 1912 model-year row.

reject_list.reason: The only stated vehicle year is outside the catalog range, and the catalog specifically excludes the 1912 launch from model-year treatment.
reject_list.evidence_text: Commons title and description: '1912 Chevrolet Series C Classic Six' and '1912 Chevrolet Classic Six at the Alfred P. Sloan Museum, Flint Michigan.' Catalog range: 1913-1914; catalog note: calendar-year 1912 launch material must not be treated as a separate model-year color row.

reject_list.reason: The photo is explicitly a 1918 FA5, outside the Baby Grand catalog range and a different series from the cataloged H-4.
reject_list.evidence_text: Commons title and description: '1918 Chevrolet Baby Grand FA5' and '1918 Chevrolet Baby Grand (Model FA5).' Catalog range: 1914-1916; catalog alias: Series H-4.

reject_list.reason: Reused-name collision: this is the later G-Series Beauville van, not the 1955-1957 Beauville station wagon.
reject_list.evidence_text: Commons description: '1988 Chevrolet Beauville.' Catalog range: 1955-1957; catalog note says the later Beauville van trim belongs under G-Series Van.

reject_list.reason: Reused-name collision: this is the later G-Series Beauville van, not the 1955-1957 Beauville station wagon.
reject_list.evidence_text: Commons filename and description: '83 Chevrolet Beauville.' Catalog range: 1955-1957; catalog note says the later Beauville van trim belongs under G-Series Van.

reject_list.reason: Detail-only image, not a vehicle view.
reject_list.evidence_text: Commons filename: '1931 Chevrolet Independence Hood Mascot 3.jpg.' Description identifies only the hood mascot.

reject_list.reason: Chassis-only image, not a complete vehicle view.
reject_list.evidence_text: Commons filename and description: 'Chevrolet copper cooled chassis.'

reject_list.reason: Detail-only hood ornament, with its only stated year also outside the catalog range.
reject_list.evidence_text: Commons filename and description: 'Chevrolet Eagle hood ornament (1932).' Catalog range: 1933 only.

reject_list.reason: The vehicle is explicitly the separately cataloged Vega Panel Express commercial delivery model, not a generic Vega representative.
reject_list.evidence_text: Commons description: '1971 Chevrolet Vega Panel Express.' Catalog has a separate vega-panel-express entry for 1971-1975 with alias 'Vega Panel.'

reject_list.reason: The photo is explicitly Concours Estate, which the catalog keeps separate from Concours.
reject_list.evidence_text: Commons filename and description identify a 1970 Chevrolet Concours Estate. The same candidate is correctly associated with concours-estate; the catalog note says brochures distinguish Concours Estate from Concours.

reject_list.reason: The photo is explicitly Kingswood Estate, which the catalog keeps separate from Kingswood.
reject_list.evidence_text: Commons filename and description identify a 1970 Chevrolet Kingswood Estate with woodgrain. The same candidate is correctly associated with kingswood-estate; the catalog note calls Kingswood Estate a separate Caprice-related flagship wagon.

reject_list.reason: The only stated model year is outside the U.S. Chevrolet Spectrum catalog range.
reject_list.evidence_text: Commons filename and description: 'Chevrolet Spectrum 1.5 1989.' Catalog range: 1985-1988.

reject_list.reason: Reused-name and market collision: this is the later global Chevrolet Classic wagon, not the 2004-2005 U.S. Malibu-derived fleet Classic.
reject_list.evidence_text: Commons filename and description: 'Chevrolet Classic 1.6 LT Wagon 2011.' Catalog range: 2004-2005; catalog notes define the U.S. entry as the fleet continuation of the prior Malibu generation.
